title: 8 Goats. 1 Jumped on a Sheriff's SUV. Gone.
script: 8 goats. 7 caught! Butler County, Ohio — Sheriff's deputies Barrett and Smith got a call: 8 goats had escaped and were trespassing in a neighbor's yard on Oxford Middletown Road. Standard stuff. They herded all 8 together, found the owner, started the handoff. 7 goats: easy. 1 goat? Not easy. That one clocked the situation, ran at Deputy Barrett's SUV, jumped ON the hood — like, full hood climber — and launched himself into the woods. Gone. The sheriff's office then posted a missing-goat bulletin describing a 'white male goat with brown markings on the right side of his face.' Not dangerous. But 'may be armed with an attitude.' So where is that 1 goat hiding in Ohio right now, and does he know he's famous?
